twitter.title: Mercedes-Benz M260/M264 Engine — Reliability

@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz models fitted with the M260/M264

mainEntity.name: How much does an M260/M264 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M260/M264 typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only [MB‑QUOTE], varying by displacement (1.5L vs 2.0L) and variant. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on labour rates.

mainEntity.name: Is the M260/M264 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M260/M264 are considered reasonably reliable in early production, earning 3.0★ out of 5 [MB‑VERIFIED] — but well-documented cylinder head failures and timing chain issues mean diligent maintenance is essential.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common M260/M264 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cylinder head failure — specifically exhaust valve seat wear leading to misfires and compression loss — typically appears between 40,000–120,000 miles. Mercedes issued extended warranties for the M260 in some markets.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes models use the M260/M264?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M260 (transverse) powers A‑Class, B‑Class, CLA, GLA, and GLB. The M264 (longitudinal) powers C‑Class, E‑Class, CLS, GLC, and G‑Class — the key difference is engine orientation.

mainEntity.name: How long does the M260/M264 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With meticulous maintenance, 160,000–190,000 miles is achievable — but high‑mileage examples often require significant expenditure on cylinder head or timing chain work. The 48V mild-hybrid system can also introduce additional complexity.

hero.tagPill: Mercedes-Benz • M260/M264 • Petrol MHEV • 1.5/2.0L Turbo I4 • 2017–present
hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M260/M264 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The M260 and M264 are Mercedes' modern turbocharged four-cylinder MHEV engines — technologically advanced but cylinder head failures are a documented weak point. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025 [MB‑VERIFIED].

rows.value: 10+ (A, B, CLA, GLA, GLB, C, E, CLS, GLC, G)

verdictRating.confidence: 85% confidence — based on cross-referenced specialist reports, owner forums, and documented warranty extensions; proprietary enquiry data pending full verification.

rows.score: 8 (Cylinder head failure often requires full head replacement (not rebuild) — major, specialist-only work)
12 (Available but cylinder heads are specialist-order items; some wait times)
TBC (Depends on host vehicle value — see R-to-V flag guidance)
TBC/100 (Sum of the five rows above)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The M260/M264 are refined, efficient modern fours — but cylinder head and timing chain failures are well-documented and expensive to fix — buy only with full service history and warranty cover.
verdictRating.bestFor: Drivers who want a modern, efficient Mercedes with the latest technology and are prepared for specialist maintenance.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You need bulletproof, low-maintenance reliability on a tight budget — older M274/M270 engines are simpler and more proven.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Cross-link: Exclusive to Mercedes-Benz — no cross-brand fitment.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M260/M264 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [MB‑QUOTE]. A cylinder head replacement alone is a major repair requiring specialist work.
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Exclusive to Mercedes-Benz — no cross-brand cost comparison applies.
